2026-05-04.content:
You can now enable zonal affinity for your Network Security Integration in-band
integration deployments and configure zonal interception with regional backends.
For more information, see [Zonal
affinity](https://docs.cloud.google.com/network-security-integration/docs/in-band/in-band-integration-overview#zonal-affinity).

summary.title: Zonal Affinity for Network Security Integration
summary.summary: You can now enable zonal affinity for your Network Security Integration in-band integration deployments. This feature allows you to configure zonal interception with regional backends, providing more control over your network traffic routing.

2026-01-14.content: You can now integrate your network security appliances directly in the network traffic path for inspection and identify any threats before the traffic reaches its destination. For more information, see [In-band integration overview](https://docs.cloud.google.com/network-security-integration/docs/in-band/in-band-integration-overview). This feature is available in **General Availability**.

summary.title: Integrate network security appliances
summary.summary: You can now integrate your network security appliances directly in the network traffic path for inspection and identify any threats before the traffic reaches its destination. This feature is available in General Availability.

2025-06-05.content: Network Security integration now retains 396 bytes for the GENEVE encapsulation overhead. Consumer networks must use the maximum transmission unit (MTU) size of 8500 bytes or less. Producer networks must use an MTU at least 396 bytes more than the consumer network. For more information, see [GENEVE encapsulation and MTU requirements](https://cloud.google.com/network-security-integration/docs/understand-geneve#geneve-mtu).

summary.title: Network Security: GENEVE Encapsulation Overhead Update
summary.summary: The GENEVE encapsulation overhead has been updated to 396 bytes. Ensure your network's MTU is set appropriately to avoid issues. Consumer networks should use an MTU of 8500 bytes or less, while producer networks need an MTU at least 396 bytes larger than the consumer network's MTU.
